curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: Alcohol was my poison. I drank Seagrams VO, a Canadian rye, with Coca-Cola. And beer. For me, beer was like air. I'd have a six-pack before noon.
quotes.speaker: Alice Cooper
quotes.context: Shock-rock legend Alice Cooper describing his heavy daily drinking before he got sober, in a Louder/Classic Rock feature on going cold turkey.

source.title: How Alice Cooper went cold turkey and made his most personal album (Louder)

quotes.text: People keep asking me how I stay so sharp at my age. I'll tell you — I avoid jogging, I avoid salad, and I avoid anyone who uses the word 'journey' to describe losing fifteen pounds. Other than that, I'm basically a goddamn physician.
quotes.speaker: George Carlin
quotes.context: Backstage Q&A session at the Beacon Theatre, New York, recorded for a DVD bonus feature, September 2007
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Carlin absolutely railed against wellness culture and self-help language, so the targets are pitch-perfect. The giveaway is the punchline is slightly too tidy for his late-career style, which got rawer and less structured than this.

quotes.text: Somebody told me the green room had a fruit platter and I nearly quit the tour on the spot. I don't travel three hundred miles on a bus that smells like a men's locker room to eat a goddamn cantaloupe wedge. Find me a burger or find me a different rider negotiator.
quotes.speaker: John Belushi
quotes.context: Captured in an audio recording during the Animal House press junket in Los Angeles, July 1978, later quoted in a Rolling Stone retrospective
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Belushi's legendary rider demands and contempt for anything health-adjacent are extremely well documented, so this lands in character. The giveaway is 'rider negotiator' — slightly too precise a term for how he'd actually have phrased it mid-rant.

quotes.text: I used to do drugs. I still do, but I used to, too.
quotes.speaker: Mitch Hedberg
quotes.context: One of late absurdist comedian Mitch Hedberg's most iconic one-liners, a paradoxical drug-use confession from his album 'Strategic Grill Locations' (1999). Hedberg's easygoing stoner persona and drug jokes were central to his act.

quotes.text: People think acting is about crying on cue and finding your motivation. Mostly it's about not laughing when someone has spinach in their teeth during a dramatic scene and knowing which craft services table has the decent whiskey.
quotes.speaker: Peter O'Toole
quotes.context: After-dinner remarks at the BAFTA Fellowship dinner, London, recorded and partially transcribed in The Guardian, February 2003
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — O'Toole's caustic, self-deprecating wit about the film industry is legendary, and his relationship with whiskey was famously part of his public persona. The giveaway is it's slightly too polished and two-part structured — his ad-libs were usually more rambling and digressive.

quotes.text: Health nuts are going to feel stupid someday, lying in hospitals dying of nothing.
quotes.speaker: Redd Foxx
quotes.context: A signature dark one-liner from the pioneering, famously raunchy stand-up comedian (star of 'Sanford and Son'), mocking clean-living health fanatics. Foxx's party-hard, cigarette-and-booze persona was legendary.

source.title: Quote Details: Redd Foxx (The Quotations Page)
